headlines.title: DOJ sets up $1.8B ‘anti-weaponization’ fund after Trump drops IRS lawsuit
headlines.description: The Justice Department announced it was establishing a $1.776 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” after President Trump moved to dismiss a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS over his leaked tax returns.

headlines.title: Rep. Thomas Massie confronts the full force of Trump’s wrath in a GOP primary
headlines.description: Rep. Thomas Massie, a constant thorn in the side of GOP leaders during his eight terms in Congress, is no stranger to a primary challenge. But this year, President Donald Trump’s fury with the Kentucky libertarian has reached a boiling point.

headlines.title: Pentagon’s internal watchdog to probe U.S. strikes on alleged drug boats
headlines.description: The inspector general will look at the intelligence and targeting Southern Command used for the strikes, as well as other aspects of the operations against drug cartels, according to a May 11 letter from the Pentagon’s Office of Inspector General.

headlines.title: Nancy Pelosi endorses fellow progressive Connie Chan in high-profile race to succeed her
headlines.description: Chan’s competition includes state Sen. Scott Weiner and wealthy former tech executive Saikat Chakrabarti, a co-founder of the progressive group Justice Democrats.

headlines.title: After primary loss, Sen. Bill Cassidy defends his 2021 vote to convict Trump
headlines.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy defended his 2021 vote to convict President Donald Trump on impeachment charges, saying he would rather be remembered for standing up for his principles even if it ended his career in the Senate.

headlines.title: Massie ‘unbothered’ as Trump tries to oust him in Kentucky GOP primary
headlines.description: NBC News correspondents Sahil Kapur, Kelly O’Donnell and Julie Tsirkin analyze the fallout from incumbent Sen. Bill Cassidy’s (R) defeat in the Louisiana primary as President Trump shifts his focus to ousting Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.) NBC News correspondent Aaron Gilchrist previews the competitive gubernatorial and Senate primaries in Georgia.

headlines.title: Threat at San Diego mosque has been neutralized, local police say
headlines.description: San Diego Police announced that the threat at a mosque in Clairemont, California has been neutralized. Police added the scene has been contained. Governor Gavin Newson has been briefed on the situation. NBC News law enforcement analyst Jim Cavanaugh breaks down the response to the shooting.

headlines.title: Justice Department sets up 'anti-weaponization fund' after Trump drops IRS lawsuit
headlines.description: The Justice Department announced that President Trump would be dropping his lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service in "exchange" for the creation of an "anti-weaponization fund." NBC News' Monica Alba reports on the announcement and how the fund could be used.

headlines.title: What GOP officials are hopeful — and worried — about six months from the midterms
headlines.description: President Donald Trump isn’t on the ballot this year, but with roughly six months before Election Day, he’ll still likely be the biggest factor in determining the fortunes of the Republican Party

headlines.title: Trump allies and Christian leaders kick off America’s 250th birthday with religious rally on National Mall
headlines.description: Thousands gathered on the National Mall on Sunday for a day of prayer, worship music and patriotic speeches for an event celebrating the nation’s 250th anniversary that supporters hail as a public affirmation of faith in America but critics view as an exclusionary display that blurs the line between religion and politics.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: News organizations sue to unseal filings in DOJ’s Fulton County election investigation
headlines.description: A group of news organizations, including NBC News, has filed a motion to unseal filings and subpoenas in the Department of Justice’s ongoing investigation into the 2020 election in Fulton County, Georgia
